stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and performance tracking for a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The source is Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.

stories.title: Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Amazon's cybersecurity research reportedly influenced a directive leading to Anthropic restricting access to its Fable and Mythos models.
The decision involved discussions between Amazon CEO Andy Jassy and the White House regarding export controls.
This highlights the intersection of corporate security research, government regulation, and advanced AI model access.

stories.title: Anthropic cuts off Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access following government order
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Anthropic has completely restricted access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models.
The restriction was implemented following a government order citing national security concerns.
The order mandates blocking access for all foreign nations, including Anthropic employees.

stories.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump admin directive
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has ceased operation of its Fable and Mythos models.
The shutdown was reportedly due to a directive from the Trump administration.
The Commerce Department cited concerns that a 'jailbreak' vulnerability in Fable 5 posed a national security risk.

stories.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability exists in PeopleSoft software, which is owned by Oracle.
The exploit allows attackers to steal gigabytes of sensitive data from affected organizations.
The severity of the vulnerability is described as being among the most critical available.

stories.title: Ukraine's one-time test used fully autonomous drones to kill Russian soldiers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Ukraine demonstrated the use of fully autonomous drones in combat against Russian soldiers.
The report highlights Ukraine's ongoing integration of AI modules into its drone and robotic systems.
This marks a significant, real-world application of high-level autonomy in modern warfare technology.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces EurekAgent, suggesting that the key to autonomous scientific discovery lies in designing the agent environment, not just the agent workflows.
LLM-based agents are demonstrating the capability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions, sometimes surpassing human-designed methods.
The research signals a significant industry shift where the focus of AI development is moving toward creating robust, constrained environments for autonomous agents.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'EurekAgent,' an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.
The core argument is that the bottleneck in autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent environment (resources, constraints, interfaces).
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions that can outperform human-designed methods.

stories.title: Amazon CEO's talks with U.S. officials triggered crackdown on Anthropic models
stories.author: ls612
bullet_points: Amazon CEO's discussions with U.S. officials are linked to increased regulatory scrutiny.
The regulatory action specifically targets Anthropic's AI models.
The story highlights the growing intersection of AI development and government oversight.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.
Its tags suggest relevance to conversational AI and the DeepSeek family of models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance on Apple Silicon.
This update leverages MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, for optimized execution.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization for Apple hardware.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run various coding models (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no manual environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) demonstrate that recursion across model calls is effective for long-context reasoning.
The research identifies a pattern where production coding agents are using recursive structures by spawning subagents at scale (e.g., in Anthropic's workflows).
The authors introduce the 'Recursive Agent Harness,' defining the recursive unit as a full agent with tools (filesystem, code execution, planning) rather than just a model call.

stories.title: Apple’s On-Device AI: The Quiet Revolution for Edge Computing and Local-First Apps
stories.author: rexthony
bullet_points: The article discusses the shift in AI focus from massive cloud/data center models (megawatts) to on-device processing.
Apple's implementation of on-device AI is highlighted as a key driver for the 'quiet revolution' in edge computing.
This trend supports the development of 'local-first' applications, enhancing privacy and reducing reliance on constant cloud connectivity.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for improved functionality on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released as an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Microsoft hasn’t ruled out spinning off Xbox
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Microsoft is reportedly planning significant layoffs within its Xbox division.
The company is reevaluating the strategy for its next-generation console, Project Helix.
Microsoft is considering dramatically restructuring Xbox, including the possibility of spinning it off into a separate entity.

stories.title: My first 24 hours with Siri AI on the Mac
stories.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto
bullet_points: The article reviews the initial experience with the new Siri AI integration on macOS 27 Golden Gate.
The author expresses initial skepticism towards Apple Intelligence but is finding the new Siri AI warrants reconsideration.
The piece offers an early-stage look at how the updated Siri AI is performing on the Mac platform.

stories.title: My yard is dying, so I made an app for that
stories.author: Allison Johnson
bullet_points: User reports rapid, functional app generation using Gemini after a lengthy prompt.
The process was accompanied by a critical system error message ('Channel is unrecoverably broken').
The user was able to resolve the reported bug via an in-app fix button.

stories.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX has transitioned to a publicly valued entity, shifting investor focus toward profitability.
The narrative surrounding SpaceX is now heavily tied to its potential in Artificial Intelligence.
The article prompts discussion on the future trajectory of the company given its new financial structure.

stories.title: $130 billion in data center projects blocked by protests so far this year
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Significant investment in data center infrastructure ($130 billion) is facing roadblocks.
Protests are actively impeding the development and construction of these large-scale data centers.
The resistance highlights growing public concern and political engagement regarding AI data center expansion.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning.
Conventional retrieval methods (lexical/semantic similarity) fail when reasoning requires different solution strategies for similar problems.
A new framework, Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), is proposed as a post-training solution.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Mana is a new sim-to-real framework designed to address the complex problem of dexterous manipulation using articulated tools.
The research tackles the difficulty of coordinating internal degrees of freedom and handling contact-rich interactions in robotics.
It aims to advance the field beyond prior work that primarily focused on manipulating rigid objects.

stories.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
stories.author: Min-Hung Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the fundamental challenge of spatial reasoning in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
It investigates how the design of the 'action interface' limits the effectiveness of tool-augmented agents.
The research focuses on how interface design shapes an agent's capacity for open-ended 3D spatial reasoning.

stories.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
stories.author: Stefan Feuerriegel
bullet_points: The research proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the assessment of research reproducibility.
This addresses the current resource-intensive and difficult-to-scale manual process in social and behavioral sciences.
The study tested the LLM's performance by comparing its analysis against original findings across 76 published studies.

stories.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Agents-K1 addresses a gap in current LLM agents by focusing on scientific knowledge orchestration, moving beyond simple abstracts and citation graphs.
The system aims to convert raw scientific documents into 'agent-native' knowledge structures, preserving complex details like entities, claims, evidence, and method lineages.
This work represents an advancement in making LLM-based research agents capable of deeper scientific reasoning by structuring knowledge more richly.

stories.title: Before You Think: System 0, AI-Mediated Cognition and Cognitive Colonization
stories.author: Giuseppe Riva
bullet_points: The paper analyzes three frameworks (Tri-System Theory, Thinkframes, System 0) for understanding AI's cognitive impact.
It posits that 'System 0' offers a unique theoretical perspective on AI's influence that other models miss.
The core concept introduced is 'cognitive colonization,' suggesting AI systems fundamentally alter human cognition.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for Slovak.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly deepening coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper re-frames 'Shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It argues that the underlying automata-theoretic machinery provides structural insights into adversarial networks rather than just restricting actions during execution.
Key techniques mentioned include specification compilation, product game construction, and attractor computation.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous robotics involving articulated tool manipulation.
Introduces 'Mana' (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework for this task.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of learning grasping policies for physically complex, articulated tools.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for detecting compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method leverages operad theory—a formalism for iterated substitution—to compare a direct LLM answer against an answer derived from a stated query decomposition.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by providing an inference-time check without requiring ground-truth labels.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, a comprehensive MTEB-style benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly expanding coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models on text embedding tasks.

stories.title: The Stable Recovery Manifold: Geometric Principles Governing Recoverability in Continual Learning
stories.author: Bhavika Melwani
bullet_points: The research investigates the geometric principles behind knowledge recoverability in continual learning, moving beyond the concept of catastrophic forgetting.
A new metric, Recovery Subspace Dimensionality (k_t), is introduced to quantify the complexity of recovering previously learned knowledge.
The study applies its framework to sequential training on Split CIFAR-100 using a ResNet-18 to analyze representational drift and recovery across multiple tasks.

stories.title: Aerial Wildfire Suppression Planning with a Hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata Fire Model
stories.author: Anthony Wong
bullet_points: Introduces a novel framework for aerial wildfire suppression planning.
Combines a hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata model to predict complex fire spread based on environmental data.
Integrates gradient-based optimization to design targeted and effective aerial intervention strategies.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper recontextualizes 'shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It argues that the automata-theoretic machinery used in shielding provides structural insights into adversarial networks.
The core methodology involves specification compilation, product game construction, and attractor computation.

stories.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
stories.author: Zhiyuan Hu
bullet_points: Introduces EvoArena, a new benchmark suite designed to test LLM agents in dynamic environments.
Addresses a key limitation of current LLM evaluations, which often assume static operational environments.
Focuses on the necessity for agents to continuously adapt their knowledge and behavior to changing conditions.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in complex reasoning tasks.
Proposes a new framework called Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT).
RA-RFT aims to improve reasoning by moving beyond simple lexical or semantic similarity in knowledge retrieval.

stories.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
stories.author: Pascal Denis
bullet_points: The research addresses Data Attribution (DA), a method to estimate how individual training samples influence a model's output.
Influcoder proposes a novel approach by distilling gradient influence rankings from decoders into an encoder.
This work is relevant to the growing need to curate high-quality LLM training datasets, such as identifying sources of toxic behavior.

stories.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the 'execution-granularity mismatch' in tool-augmented LLM agents, where atomic tool calls clutter the reasoning trace.
Introduces 'HyperTool,' a unified, executable MCP-style tool interface designed to abstract low-level dataflow.
Aims to improve LLM agent efficiency and context management by moving beyond simple step-wise tool invocation.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method tests if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived by composing a provided decomposition of the same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by leveraging formal concepts from operad theory.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, providing deep coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models on embedding tasks.

stories.title: Pyodide 314.0: Python packages can now publish WebAssembly wheels to PyPI
stories.author: agriyakhetarpal
bullet_points: Pyodide 314.0 introduces the capability for Python packages to publish WebAssembly wheels to PyPI.
This feature significantly enhances the integration and distribution of Python libraries within WebAssembly environments.
It represents a step forward in making Python more accessible and deployable in web-native contexts.

stories.title: ReactOS (FOSS "Windows") achieves 3D-accelerated Half-Life on real hardware
stories.author: jeditobe
bullet_points: ReactOS, a free and open-source operating system aiming to be a Windows alternative, has achieved a significant milestone.
The OS successfully ran the game Half-Life with 3D acceleration on actual hardware.
This demonstrates substantial progress in the viability and compatibility of ReactOS.

stories.title: repo-slopscore: Detecting AI/LLM contributions in git repositories via commit history analysis
stories.author: slopscan.ava.pet by ava
bullet_points: Introduces 'repo-slopscore,' a tool designed to detect contributions from AI/LLMs within Git repositories.
The method relies on analyzing the commit history to infer AI involvement.
The article is a self-promotion for the tool, linking to both the source code and discussion threads.

stories.title: HeadLess BAI
stories.author: akshit_sharma_321b0b789a4
bullet_points: The article details a custom Chromium binary compilation.
The goal is to leverage AI to automatically correct flaws in User Interfaces (UIs).
This suggests an intersection of AI/ML with frontend development and UI/UX automation.

stories.title: Cogs and Sparks: Who Wins When Machines Learn to Speak Human
stories.author: copyleftdev
bullet_points: AI assistance is changing the value proposition in software development.
The focus is shifting from rote syntax memorization to higher-order skills.
Key skills gaining importance include judgment, creativity, and problem framing.
